# Button

<!-- Generated by @create-component-md. Source of truth: Figma → .md migration. -->
<!-- Figma: https://www.figma.com/design/QJ0f6PWjR3ERLtDVz652b5/?node-id=3062-395 -->
<!-- Generated at: 2026-06-05T16:46:30.938Z -->
<!-- Optional context provided: none -->

## Overview

Button is Blueprint's interactive action control. Its surface is driven by three variant axes — Appearance (Primary, Secondary, Tertiary, Subdued), Color (Default, Inverse, Accent), and a State axis that decomposes to the single persistent API prop `isDisabled` (Hover, Press, and Focus are transient runtime states). The control hosts an editable text label and two optional, independent icon slots — a leading and a trailing icon — each gated by a `has*` boolean and filled by an instance-swap of an Icon component. Dimensions are carried by the Appearance axis (Primary/Secondary enforce an 88 min-width and token-bound padding; Tertiary/Subdued read as borderless text-style buttons), and every fill and stroke binds to a Foundations color token. The accessible name merges the visible label; the icons are decorative and hidden from assistive tech.

**Variant axes.** Appearance (Primary, Secondary, Tertiary, Subdued; default Primary) · Color (Default, Inverse, Accent; default Default) · State (Default, Hover, Press, Focus, Disabled; default Default).

### Composition

- **arrowRotateLeft** (referenced — arrowRotateLeft) — configured inline below; full spec at `./arrowrotateleft.md` (when present).
- **download** (referenced — download) — configured inline below; full spec at `./download.md` (when present).
- _Decorative children: 2 — documented inline in Structure and Color._

## API

_Confidence: high._

> State axis is decomposed: only the persistent Disabled state is exposed as isDisabled; Hover, Press, and Focus are transient interaction states handled by the platform at runtime and are not API properties. Leading and trailing icons are independent, optional slots: each is gated by a has* boolean and its glyph is chosen via an instance-swap of an Icon component. No local variable collections drive shape or density.

### Properties

| Property | Type | Values | Default | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| appearance | enum | Primary \| Secondary \| Tertiary \| Subdued | Primary | Visual hierarchy / emphasis. Figma axis name: Appearance. |
| color | enum | Default \| Inverse \| Accent | Default | Color treatment. Inverse for dark or colored surfaces; Accent for brand-accent emphasis. |
| isDisabled | boolean | true \| false | false | Persistent disabled state. Decomposed from the Figma State axis; Hover/Press/Focus are transient and not exposed. |
| label | string | (string) | - | Button text. In Figma, mirrored from the Label editable text property (default "Label"). |
| hasLeadingIcon | boolean | true \| false | false | Shows a leading icon before the label. |
| leadingIcon | instance-swap | (instance) | arrowRotateLeft | Only meaningful when hasLeadingIcon = true. Icon rendered when hasLeadingIcon=true. Instance-swap of an Icon component. |
| hasTrailingIcon | boolean | true \| false | false | Shows a trailing icon after the label. |
| trailingIcon | instance-swap | (instance) | download | Only meaningful when hasTrailingIcon = true. Icon rendered when hasTrailingIcon=true. Instance-swap of an Icon component. |

### Leading icon

Instance of Icon, gated by hasLeadingIcon. See Icon API for full details.

| Property | Type | Values | Default | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| icon | instance-swap | (instance) | arrowRotateLeft | Glyph selected via the leadingIcon instance-swap. |
| size | inherited | inherited | - | Sized to the button label; not independently configurable from the parent. |

### Trailing icon

Instance of Icon, gated by hasTrailingIcon. See Icon API for full details.

| Property | Type | Values | Default | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| icon | instance-swap | (instance) | download | Glyph selected via the trailingIcon instance-swap. |
| size | inherited | inherited | - | Sized to the button label; not independently configurable from the parent. |

### Example 1 - Primary button

| Property | Value | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| appearance | primary | Default emphasis |
| label | "Get started" | - |

### Example 2 - Secondary with leading icon

| Property | Value | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| appearance | secondary | - |
| hasLeadingIcon | true | Shows leading icon |
| leadingIcon | arrowRotateLeft | Default leading glyph |
| label | "Refresh" | - |

### Example 3 - Tertiary with trailing icon

| Property | Value | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| appearance | tertiary | - |
| hasTrailingIcon | true | Shows trailing icon |
| trailingIcon | download | Default trailing glyph |
| label | "Download" | - |

### Example 4 - Disabled primary

| Property | Value | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| isDisabled | true | Disabled state (Figma State=Disabled) |
| label | "Unavailable" | - |

## Structure

_Confidence: high._

> Button has no size axis; its dimensional differences are carried by the Appearance axis (Primary / Secondary / Tertiary / Subdued), which is used as the column axis. Color (Default / Inverse / Accent) is visual-only — identical dimensions across all three values. State (Default / Hover / Press / Focus / Disabled) is dimensionally constant except for two root-border changes: the Secondary outline thickens on Hover (4) and Press (5), and Tertiary / Subdued gain a 2px container-stroke focus ring on Focus (both documented in State deltas and the Button — Focus section). Horizontal padding (32) and vertical padding (8) bind to spacing tokens (spacing/fluid/ml, spacing/fluid/xxxs); Tertiary and Subdued drop horizontal padding to 0 and use vertical padding 4 (spacing/fixed/xxxs) — they read as text/link-style buttons with no minWidth floor. Root borders: only Secondary paints a persistent root border (2px outline, thickening to 4 on Hover and 5 on Press); Primary is a solid fill with no root border, and Tertiary / Subdued have no root border at rest (their underline is a separate Bottom border child - see Color). Focus rings are not root borders except on Tertiary / Subdued, which gain a 2px container stroke on Focus; Primary and Secondary focus rings are a separate Focus indicator element (see Color). Secondary's border weights are not bound to a width token - consider binding them. The inner Content frame reports strokeWeight 1 with no paint applied (strokes:null) — treated as a non-painted Figma artifact on a layout wrapper, so no border row is emitted for it.

### State deltas

_Non-dimensional properties that change across the State axis. Dimensional values are documented in the sections below._

| Element | rest (enabled) | hovered (transient, runtime) | pressed (transient, runtime) | focused (transient, runtime) | disabled | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Secondary root border strokeWeight | 2 | 4 | 5 | 2 | 2 | Secondary's outline thickens on Hover (4) and Press (5); rest / focus / disabled stay 2. Shown for the Secondary appearance - Primary has no root border; Tertiary / Subdued gain a 2px container-stroke focus ring on Focus only (see Button — Focus). |

### Button appearance

Button has no size axis; the Appearance axis carries the dimensional differences and is used as the columns. Primary and Secondary are full-padding buttons (32 horizontal); Tertiary and Subdued are text/link-style with no horizontal padding, reduced vertical padding (4), and no minWidth. Color does not change these dimensions; State changes only the Secondary root border (Hover / Press) and the Tertiary / Subdued focus ring (see State deltas and Button — Focus).

| Spec | Primary | Secondary | Tertiary | Subdued | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Container | – | – | – | – | Root button frame — tap target and content host |
| ├ minHeight | 44 | 44 | 44 | 44 | Constant 44 tap-target height across all appearances — meets WCAG 2.5.8 minimum |
| ├ minWidth | 88 | 88 | – | – | Primary/Secondary enforce an 88 minimum width; Tertiary/Subdued hug their label with no width floor |
| ├ horizontalPadding | spacing/fluid/ml (32) | spacing/fluid/ml (32) | 0 | 0 | Token-bound inset on Primary/Secondary; Tertiary/Subdued have zero horizontal padding (text/link style) |
| ├ verticalPadding | spacing/fluid/xxxs (8) | spacing/fluid/xxxs (8) | spacing/fixed/xxxs (4) | spacing/fixed/xxxs (4) | Reduced to 4 on Tertiary/Subdued — smaller vertical inset suits the borderless text treatment |
| ├ itemSpacing | spacing/fluid/xxxs (8) | spacing/fluid/xxxs (8) | spacing/fluid/xxxs (8) | spacing/fluid/xxxs (8) | Constant 8 gap between leading icon, label, and trailing icon |
| ├ cornerRadius | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | Square corners across all appearances |
| ├ borderWidth | – | 2 | – | – | Only Secondary paints a persistent root border (2px outline); it thickens on Hover (4) and Press (5) - see State deltas. Primary is a solid fill with no root border; Tertiary / Subdued have none at rest (their underline is a separate Bottom border child - see Color). Not bound to a width token. |
| └ borderAlign | – | inside | – | – | Secondary's outline is painted inside the frame edge - does not expand the bounding box; other appearances have no root border at rest |

### Content

Inner horizontal auto-layout frame hosting the leading icon, label, and trailing icon. Both icons are referenced library instances (arrowRotateLeft / download), gated by the hasLeadingIcon / hasTrailingIcon booleans and hidden by default. See the icon component's own spec for internals.

| Spec | Default | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Content | – | Inner content frame — hugs its children both axes |
| ├ itemSpacing | spacing/fluid/xxxs (8) | Gap between leading icon, label, and trailing icon |
| ├ paddingTop | 1 | 1px optical-alignment offset to center the label cap-height; other sides 0 |
| └ widthMode | hug | Hugs the combined width of its children |
| Leading icon | – | Referenced library instance; hidden unless hasLeadingIcon is true |
| ├ leadingIcon | arrowRotateLeft | Default swap target (arrowRotateLeft); swappable via the leadingIcon instance-swap property |
| └ leadingIconSize | 16 | Fixed 16 square icon frame. See icon spec for internals |
| Label | – | Editable text node — content driven by the Label property |
| └ textStyle | Body/M Bold | Gotham Bold 16 / line-height 26 |
| Trailing icon | – | Referenced library instance; hidden unless hasTrailingIcon is true |
| ├ trailingIcon | download | Default swap target (download); swappable via the trailingIcon instance-swap property |
| └ trailingIconSize | 16 | Fixed 16 square icon frame. See icon spec for internals |

### Button — Focus

Focus rings are not all root borders. On Primary and Secondary the focus ring is a separate Focus indicator element (documented in Color), so the root border does not change on Focus. On Tertiary and Subdued the focus ring is a 2px container stroke painted on the root, present only in the Focus state. All other dimensions are unchanged.

| Spec | Default | Focus | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Container | – | – | Root button frame |
| ├ borderWidth | – | 2 | Tertiary / Subdued gain a 2px container-stroke focus ring on Focus only (shown here); absent in rest / hover / press / disabled. Primary / Secondary have no root-border change on Focus - their focus ring is a separate Focus indicator element (see Color). |
| └ borderAlign | – | inside | Painted inside the frame edge - does not enlarge the tap target |

---

## Color

_Confidence: high._

> Color (Default, Inverse, Accent) and Appearance (Primary, Secondary, Tertiary, Subdued) are traditional Figma variant axes, not a variable mode collection. Primary buttons render hover and pressed feedback via opacity/* state-overlay primitives on the container fill. Tertiary and Subdued use a bottom-border underline; the keyboard focus ring is a container stroke (Tertiary, Subdued) or a dedicated Focus indicator (Primary, Secondary). Leading and Trailing icon fills are owned by the button.

| Container fill | button/primary/surface (#25273A) | opacity/white-20 (#FFFFFF) | opacity/white-40 (#FFFFFF) | button/primary/surface (#25273A) | surface/disabled (#CACACA) | Button surface fill |
| Focus indicator | none | none | none | button/primary/surface (#25273A) | none | Keyboard focus ring |
| Leading icon | icon/inverse (#FFFFFF) | icon/inverse (#FFFFFF) | icon/inverse (#FFFFFF) | icon/inverse (#FFFFFF) | icon/subtler (#666666) | Leading icon fill |
| Label | text/inverse (#FFFFFF) | text/inverse (#FFFFFF) | text/inverse (#FFFFFF) | text/inverse (#FFFFFF) | text/subtler (#666666) | Button text label |
| Trailing icon | icon/inverse (#FFFFFF) | icon/inverse (#FFFFFF) | icon/inverse (#FFFFFF) | icon/inverse (#FFFFFF) | icon/subtler (#666666) | Trailing icon fill |

## Voice / Screen reader

_Confidence: high._

> Button is a single focus stop. The visible Label text merges into the button's accessible name and is never exposed as a separate element. Leading and trailing icons (toggled by hasLeadingIcon / hasTrailingIcon) are decorative — they reinforce the label's meaning and must be hidden from assistive tech; they never add a second focus stop. The Figma State axis (Default, Hover, Press, Focus, Disabled) decomposes to one persistent API state, isDisabled: Hover, Press, and Focus are transient runtime states with identical screen-reader semantics to the enabled rest state, so they are grouped into a single enabled entry. Disabled removes the button from the focus order entirely (focus stop count = 0); document .notEnabled / disabled() / aria-disabled but list no focus order entries. Label the action, not the appearance ('Submit', not 'Blue button'). iOS: 'dimmed' is appended automatically for disabled; do not bake it into the label. Web: prefer native <button> over role='button' so Enter/Space activation and disabled handling come for free. Do not reorder focus across states — the button is always a single stop. Do not stuff the accessible name with state; let traits / role / aria-disabled carry it.

### State: rest (enabled)

Default resting button, also covers the transient hovered / pressed / focused runtime states (Figma State = Default / Hover / Press / Focus) — all share identical screen-reader semantics. isDisabled = false.

#### VoiceOver (iOS)

| Property | Value | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Announcement | "Label, button" |  |
| accessibilityLabel | "Label" | Spoken name, taken from the merged visible Label text. Replace with the real action verb in implementation. |
| accessibilityTraits | .isButton | Declares the element as an interactive button so VoiceOver announces the role and the activation gesture. |
| accessibilityHint | – | Omit unless the result of activation is non-obvious; the label already conveys the action. |
| Do NOT | expose Label as a separate element | Do not read the label twice. The Label text merges into the button's name; do not expose the Label node as its own focusable element. |
| Do NOT | announce leading/trailing icon | Do not announce the decorative icon. Mark it accessibilityElementsHidden = true; it duplicates the label's meaning. |

#### TalkBack (Android)

| Property | Value | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Announcement | "Label, button, double-tap to activate" |  |
| contentDescription | "Label" | Spoken label, merged from the visible Label text via mergeDescendants on the button container. |
| role | Role.Button | Maps to the button role so TalkBack appends 'button' and the 'double-tap to activate' hint. |
| Do NOT | expose Label as a separate element | Do not read the label twice. Set importantForAccessibility = no on the Label so it merges into the button rather than being a separate focusable node. |
| Do NOT | announce leading/trailing icon | Do not announce the decorative icon. Set contentDescription = null / importantForAccessibility = no on the icon. |

#### ARIA (Web)

| Property | Value | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Announcement | "Label, button" |  |
| element | <button> | Prefer native <button>; the role 'button' and keyboard (Enter/Space) activation are implicit. |
| textContent | "Label" | Visible button text supplies the accessible name; no aria-label needed when text is present. |
| Do NOT | add aria-label duplicating the text | Do not use aria-label to repeat the visible Label; the text content already names the button and aria-label would override it. |
| Do NOT | announce leading/trailing icon | Do not announce the decorative icon. Set aria-hidden = true on the icon SVG; it duplicates the label's meaning. |

### State: disabled

Non-interactive button (Figma State = Disabled). isDisabled = true. The button is removed from the focus order entirely — zero focus stops — but its inert semantics are still documented.

#### VoiceOver (iOS)

| Property | Value | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Announcement | "Label, dimmed, button" |  |
| accessibilityTraits | .notEnabled | Marks the button as unavailable; VoiceOver appends 'dimmed' and the user cannot activate it. |
| accessibilityLabel | "Label" | Name is unchanged; do not bake 'disabled' or 'dimmed' into the label string. |
| Do NOT | keep the stop focusable when disabled | Do not keep the button in the focus order when disabled — remove it and set .notEnabled rather than leaving it tappable. |

#### TalkBack (Android)

| Property | Value | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Announcement | "Label, button, disabled" |  |
| disabled() | applied | Adds the disabled state to semantics so TalkBack announces 'disabled' and blocks activation. |
| role | Role.Button | Role is retained so the element is still identified as a button while inert. |
| Do NOT | keep onClick active when disabled | Do not leave a clickable action wired up — remove onClick and call disabled() so the node drops out of the focus order. |

#### ARIA (Web)

| Property | Value | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Announcement | "Label, button, dimmed/unavailable" |  |
| element | <button disabled> | Native disabled attribute removes the button from the tab order and prevents activation. |
| aria-disabled | true | Use only if the design must keep the element focusable for discoverability; otherwise the native disabled attribute is preferred. |
| Do NOT | keep the stop focusable when disabled | Do not leave the disabled button in the keyboard tab order; the native disabled attribute already removes it. |

<!-- voice-render-meta v=1
{"focusStops":[{"name":"Button","focusOrderIndex":1,"layerName":"Button","slotIndex":null},{"name":"Button (disabled)","focusOrderIndex":1,"layerName":null,"slotIndex":null}]}
-->
